calculus-volume-3:c1s1.reason: Parametrics review continues Calc 2 parametric equations.

calculus-volume-3:c2s4.reason: Cross product is a 3D vector operation.

calculus-volume-3:c2s5.reason: Lines and planes use vector operations.

calculus-volume-3:c2s6.reason: Quadric surfaces extend conic ideas to 3D.

calculus-volume-3:c2s7.reason: Cylindrical/spherical coords combine 3D vectors with polar ideas.

calculus-volume-3:c3s1.reason: Vector-valued functions map parameters to vectors/curves.

calculus-volume-3:c3s2.reason: Calculus of vector functions uses componentwise derivatives.

calculus-volume-3:c3s4.reason: Motion in space interprets vector derivatives as velocity/acceleration.

calculus-volume-3:c4s1.reason: Functions of several variables extend single-variable functions to R^n.

calculus-volume-3:c4s3.reason: Partial derivatives differentiate in one variable at a time.

calculus-volume-3:c4s5.reason: Multivariable chain rule extends single-variable chain rule.

calculus-volume-3:c4s6.reason: Gradient/directional derivatives use partials and dot products.

calculus-volume-3:c4s8.reason: Lagrange multipliers constrain multivariable extrema with gradients.

calculus-volume-3:c5s1.reason: Double integrals over rectangles extend definite integrals to 2D.

calculus-volume-3:c5s2.reason: General-region double integrals extend rectangular ones.

calculus-volume-3:c5s4.reason: Triple integrals extend double integrals to 3D.

calculus-volume-3:c5s5.reason: Cylindrical/spherical triple integrals use those coordinate systems.

calculus-volume-3:c5s6.reason: Moments/centers of mass use multiple integrals.

calculus-volume-3:c5s7.reason: Change of variables generalizes polar/cylindrical substitutions.

calculus-volume-3:c6s1.reason: Vector fields assign vectors to points in space.

calculus-volume-3:c6s2.reason: Line integrals integrate along curves in vector fields.

calculus-volume-3:c6s3.reason: Conservative fields connect line integrals to gradients.

calculus-volume-3:c6s4.reason: Green's Theorem relates line integrals to double integrals.

calculus-volume-3:c6s5.reason: Divergence and curl are differential operators on vector fields.

calculus-volume-3:c6s6.reason: Surface integrals integrate over surfaces in fields.

calculus-volume-3:c6s7.reason: Stokes' Theorem relates curl to surface/line integrals.

calculus-volume-3:c6s8.reason: Divergence Theorem relates divergence to flux integrals.

calculus-volume-3:c7s1.reason: Second-order linear DEs extend first-order DE methods.

calculus-volume-3:c7s2.reason: Nonhomogeneous equations build on homogeneous second-order theory.

calculus-volume-3:c7s3.reason: Applications use second-order DE solution methods.

calculus-volume-3:c7s4.reason: Series solutions of DEs use power/Taylor series.
